response.justification: The candidate is a broadly experienced full-stack and systems developer, but he misses nearly every core requirement of this role: no native-level Japanese (a hard requirement), no Layer-2/Layer-3 networking or LAN-switch background, no security-technology experience (MACsec, 802.1X, authentication systems), and no demonstrated Linux kernel investigation or modification work. His C experience is brief and dated (a seven-month stint in 2014-2015), with most of his career in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang for web platforms. The mismatch is fundamental rather than marginal.
strengths: 15+ years of software development experience, far exceeding the 3-year minimum
Some systems-level exposure: C/C++ components at National Instruments, DLang low-latency services, Linux-based audio server project
Strong engineering practices (TDD, CI/CD) and open-source contribution track record
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed, which is a non-negotiable requirement for this role
No experience with L2/L3 networking, network switches, or the listed security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal)
C for UNIX experience is minimal and dated, with no evidence of Linux kernel analysis or source-code modification

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security domain experience
Weak, dated C/UNIX and no Linux kernel experience
